title: Gateway proxy panics on nil response body
author: contributor-alice

assessments.reasoning: Nil pointer dereference on response body is a crash-level defect. The proxy does not check for nil before reading.
The nil check is missing in the proxy handler. This violates the error handling convention (CS-006) which requires checking all return values.
The reporter expects the proxy to handle upstream failures gracefully, but the current design intentionally surfaces upstream errors. This is a design philosophy difference.
A panic in production crashes the process without cleanup. This is an operational reliability issue regardless of design intent.
No test covers the nil response body path. This is a test coverage gap that allowed a crash-level defect to ship.

dissenting_agents.reasoning: Considers this a design philosophy difference rather than a defect
